metadata.title: Build the Task Queue Pattern with Claude: Parallel Work Without Context Bloat
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Every tangential idea that fires during a focused build is a choice: break focus or lose the idea. The task queue is a third option.

The task queue relocates supervision — it does not eliminate it. Every return that is not evaluated is a silent failure in waiting. The discipline is two rules: only fire what you will return to, and evaluate every return before the next session ends. Unevaluated task queue returns are the ambient debt of the parallel-work pattern.
Fire a background task queue item: investigate whether average submission length is useful. Return with a yes or no recommendation and estimated implementation time.

props.command: claude "TASK QUEUE ITEM: Investigate whether adding average-submission-length (in lines) to the cohort pattern report is useful. Read the 5 sample reports in ./reports/sample/ and return: yes/no recommendation with 2 specific reasons and an estimated implementation time in lines of code."

beats.narration_text: The pattern: main session in left pane continuing primary build; background claude call in right pane with task queue prompt; return appears; evaluation pass adds USE, REVISE, or REVERT label and one-line rationale.

props.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Task queue: fire background investigation
# while main session continues unaffected

# RIGHT pane (background):
claude "TASK QUEUE ITEM: ..." > tq_return.md &

# LEFT pane: continue primary build normally
# (no shared context, no file writes from tq)

# When return arrives — evaluate before next session:
# Read tq_return.md
# Label: USE | REVISE | REVERT
# One-line rationale
# Example:
# REVISE: finding useful but recommendation wrong;
# implement line-count variance, not average.

beats.narration_text: Task queue return: No — average submission length is a noisy signal; cohort variance in line count is more informative. Implementation: 15 lines. Evaluation: REVISE — implement line-count variance, not average. Main session context unaffected.
Demonstrate fire-and-forget failure: skip evaluation, show the cost in the next session.

props.command:
# next session starts — unevaluated tq_return.md
# sits in context
claude "Continue the grading tool build."
# Codex reads unevaluated task queue item
# and begins implementing the excluded feature

beats.narration_text: Unevaluated task queue return from previous session: a recommendation to add a feature the AGENTS.md explicitly excludes — grade book integration. Without evaluation, the next session's context included the item and Codex began implementing the excluded feature. Task queue items are not decoration — they are inputs to the next session.

Two rules: only fire what you will return to, and evaluate every return before the next session ends. The task queue item is a read-only investigation — it never modifies the main session. The evaluation is the supervisory work: USE, REVISE, REVERT. The label and one-line rationale keep the queue from accumulating silent debt.
Next: write the post-build document that converts the build experience into teaching capacity.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. At NikBearBrown, nikbearbrown dot com.
